required_inputs: request scope and intended housing finance action
specialty boundary and adjacent-owner map
funding stack, approval matrix, and authority context
refusal_triggers: request asks for rent, subsidy, HAP, HOME draw, development draw, LIHTC, accounting, tax, legal, write-off, owner distribution, reserve release, lender, audit, or financial approval authority
request asks the lane to alter official certifications, voucher records, draw records, ledgers, GL, audit records, or compliance records without authority and traceability

required_inputs: HUD, eCFR, IRS, 2 CFR Part 200, GAO, HFA, lender, and tenant source set
source freshness metadata
tenant source hierarchy and program overlay
refusal_triggers: required authoritative housing finance source context is missing
stale source context would change charge, HAP, draw, posting, reconciliation, or exception handling
public sources, tenant policy, lender instructions, or program manuals conflict materially

required_inputs: leases, certifications, HAP, TRACS, ledger, GL, bank, HOME, IDIS, draw, LIHTC, FASS, and audit artifacts
systems-of-record links and retrieval hooks
financial artifact checklist and acceptance evidence
refusal_triggers: critical official records or audit trails are absent, stale, or contradictory
billing, posting, draw, or adjustment support cannot be traced to authoritative housing records
destination-owner acceptance and artifact completeness cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
owner map across property management, subsidy, grants, development accounting, lender, compliance, tax, accounting, legal, and audit systems
posting, reconciliation, approval, blocker, close, and escalation criteria
refusal_triggers: authoritative system or record location is unknown
critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems materially disagree on certification, HAP, tenant ledger, draw, grant, lender, tax-credit, bank, GL, or close state

required_inputs: missing tenant fact flags
source and policy conflict indicators
authority completeness signals
variance and denial indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authoritative records would change the financial output
source, policy, accounting, tax, legal, lender, subsidy, grant, compliance, or owner conflict prevents safe synthesis
correct adjacent owner or approving authority cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: adjacent specialist registry
handoff prerequisites and artifact requirements
decision owner map
authority boundary
refusal_triggers: request belongs to occupancy compliance, subsidy contract administration, asset management, grant management, development accounting, lender servicing, LIHTC compliance, tax, legal, controller, audit, or HUD/PHA approval ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff evidence is incomplete or current owner is not accountable

required_inputs: housing finance trigger such as rent charge, HAP voucher, grant draw, reserve release, development invoice, receivable posting, adjustment, denial, or close blocker
source records and systems that establish the trigger
coding, chart-of-account, period, amount, property, project, tenant, unit, grant, or lender context
refusal_triggers: financial trigger is not supported by authoritative housing records
coding, classification, period, property, project, tenant, grant, or source hierarchy is missing
requested trigger depends on approval or interpretation outside the lane

required_inputs: matched and unmatched financial records across housing systems
variance, denial, reject, dispute, aging, close blocker, and exception state
root-cause categories and escalation criteria
refusal_triggers: variance cannot be reconciled to authoritative records
denial, reject, dispute, write-off, draw, or close blocker requires another owner
systems materially disagree and no approved source hierarchy resolves the difference

required_inputs: requested financial change, adjustment, write-off, override, draw, distribution, or accounting treatment
source support and approval evidence
tenant policy and authority matrix
refusal_triggers: requested change lacks authoritative source support
requested change requires financial approval, accounting, tax, legal, lender, grantor, HUD, PHA, or compliance authority
request asks to bypass documented controls, source hierarchy, or audit trail

required_inputs: housing-specific terminology, funding stack, program type, and artifact set
official records from subsidy, property, grant, development, lender, tax-credit, GL, bank, and audit systems
tenant source hierarchy and public baseline source context
refusal_triggers: industry-specific housing artifacts are missing or treated as cosmetic labels
request cannot be grounded in actual housing records and systems
tenant-specific labels cannot be mapped to the public baseline without assumptions
